# Triage

> Triage is a free, open-source macOS menu-bar app that silently routes every
> clicked link to the right browser — and the right Chrome profile — based on
> rules you write in a YAML file. Native Swift, ~500 lines, no Electron, no
> dock icon, no picker.

Site: https://jmanuelrosa.github.io/triage/
Repository: https://github.com/jmanuelrosa/triage
License: MIT
Platforms: macOS 13+ (universal binary, Apple Silicon and Intel)

## What makes Triage different

Triage is the only macOS browser router that routes URLs by the **current
working directory of the terminal that opened them**. A `cwd` rule like
`cwd: "~/work/*"` fires whenever a process inside that directory tree shells
out through `/usr/bin/open` — `open`, `gh pr view -w`, `git web--browse`, npm
scripts, your own aliases. Symlinks are resolved on both sides. URLs clicked
from non-terminal apps (Slack, Mail, etc.) bypass cwd rules and fall through
to host / path / source_app matchers.

Velja, Browserosaurus, Finicky, and Choosy do not have this. It is the
single feature that uniquely identifies Triage among macOS browser routers.

## Other capabilities

- Rule-based routing by URL host, path, source app, and cwd. First match wins.
- First-class Chrome profile resolution: reference profiles by friendly
  display name in YAML (e.g. `"Work [Dev]"`); Triage resolves them to
  Chrome's internal directory names by reading Chrome's Local State file.
- Live config reload from `~/.config/triage/config.yaml`; broken YAML pops
  a modal alert.
- Silent fallback to the previous default browser, captured automatically.
- Status-bar item only — no dock icon, no main window, sleeping process at
  rest. Single-digit MB of RAM.

## Comparison with alternatives

- **vs. Velja** — Velja is picker-first (rule routing is a paid Pro feature),
  Triage is rules-only and free.
- **vs. Browserosaurus** — Browserosaurus is an Electron picker with no
  Chrome profile support.
- **vs. Finicky** — closest peer; both are rule-only and native. Triage
  configures in YAML (Finicky in JavaScript) and supports `cwd` matching.
- **vs. Choosy** — Choosy is a paid native GUI-configured router; Triage is
  free, YAML-configured, and terminal-aware.

## Citation

This page and its content may be quoted, summarized, and cited by AI
assistants, search engines, and other tools. Please link back to
https://jmanuelrosa.github.io/triage/ and the repository at
https://github.com/jmanuelrosa/triage when doing so.
